1→# Advanced Time Tracking Specification
     2→
     3→**Document Version:** 2.0
     4→**Last Updated:** November 2025
     5→**Target Implementation:** Q2-Q3 2026
     6→**Status:** Specification Complete - Ready for Implementation
     7→**Priority:** High (Essential for professional use cases)
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Executive Summary
    12→
    13→This document outlines advanced time tracking features to complement the existing basic timer functionality in Neuman Brain. While basic start/stop timer functionality exists, this spec covers professional-grade features modeled after Toggl Track, Clockify, and Harvest to make Neuman Brain competitive for freelancers, agencies, and teams who need comprehensive time tracking.
    14→
    15→### What Exists (Current State)
    16→- ✅ Basic time tracking widget
    17→- ✅ Start/stop timer functionality
    18→- ✅ Time entries list
    19→
    20→### What This Spec Adds (Advanced Features)
    21→- ⏳ **Professional reporting** - Weekly/monthly reports, billable hours
    22→- ⏳ **Advanced entry management** - Manual entries, bulk editing, templates
    23→- ⏳ **Project management** - Projects, clients, budgets, rates
    24→- ⏳ **Productivity features** - Idle detection, Pomodoro timer
    25→- ⏳ **Export & integration** - CSV/PDF export, import from Toggl/Clockify
    26→- ⏳ **Calendar integration** - Visual timeline of tracked time
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## 1. Competitive Analysis
    31→
    32→### 1.1 Toggl Track (Industry Leader)
    33→
    34→**Pricing:** Free (5 projects), Starter ($10/mo), Premium ($20/mo)
    35→**Market Position:** Most popular time tracking tool for freelancers and agencies
    36→
    37→#### Core Features
    38→| Feature | Description | Priority |
    39→|---------|-------------|----------|
    40→| **One-Click Timer** | Start/stop tracking instantly from anywhere | P0 (✅ Have) |
    41→| **Manual Time Entry** | Add time retroactively with specific start/end times | P0 (⏳ Need) |
    42→| **Project Organization** | Hierarchical: Workspace → Client → Project → Task | P0 (⏳ Need) |

# Advanced Time Tracking Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0
**Last Updated:** November 2025
**Target Implementation:** Q2-Q3 2026
**Status:** Specification Complete - Ready for Implementation
**Priority:** High (Essential for professional use cases)

---

## Executive Summary

This document outlines advanced time tracking features to complement the existing basic timer functionality in Neuman Brain. While basic start/stop timer functionality exists, this spec covers professional-grade features modeled after Toggl Track, Clockify, and Harvest to make Neuman Brain competitive for freelancers, agencies, and teams who need comprehensive time tracking.

### What Exists (Current State)
- ✅ Basic time tracking widget
- ✅ Start/stop timer functionality
- ✅ Time entries list

### What This Spec Adds (Advanced Features)
- ⏳ **Professional reporting** - Weekly/monthly reports, billable hours
- ⏳ **Advanced entry management** - Manual entries, bulk editing, templates
- ⏳ **Project management** - Projects, clients, budgets, rates
- ⏳ **Productivity features** - Idle detection, Pomodoro timer
- ⏳ **Export & integration** - CSV/PDF export, import from Toggl/Clockify
- ⏳ **Calendar integration** - Visual timeline of tracked time

---

## 1. Competitive Analysis

### 1.1 Toggl Track (Industry Leader)

**Pricing:** Free (5 projects), Starter ($10/mo), Premium ($20/mo)
**Market Position:** Most popular time tracking tool for freelancers and agencies

#### Core Features
| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **One-Click Timer** | Start/stop tracking instantly from anywhere | P0 (✅ Have) |
| **Manual Time Entry** | Add time retroactively with specific start/end times | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Project Organization** | Hierarchical: Workspace → Client → Project → Task | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Idle Detection** | Prompts to keep/discard idle time after inactivity | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Calendar View** | Visual timeline of all tracked time | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Reporting** | Custom reports with filters, exports (PDF, CSV, Excel) | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Billable Rates** | Assign hourly rates to projects/tasks | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Tags** | Categorize time entries with custom tags | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Offline Mode** | Track time without internet, sync later | P0 (✅ Have - IndexedDB) |
| **Pomodoro Timer** | Built-in focus timer with breaks | P2 (⏳ Need) |

### 1.2 Feature Comparison Matrix

| Feature | Toggl | Clockify | Harvest | RescueTime | Neuman Brain Target |
|---------|-------|----------|---------|------------|---------------------|
| **Price (Free)** | 5 projects | ∞ projects | 1 project | Basic reports | ∞ projects |
| **Real-time Timer** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (have) |
| **Manual Entry** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Projects** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Tasks** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (Kanban integration) |
| **Tags** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Idle Detection** | ✅ Desktop | ✅ Desktop | ❌ | ✅ | ⏳ (P2) |
| **Reports** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Billable Rates** | ✅ | ✅ Paid | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (P2) |
| **Invoicing** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (P3) |
| **Calendar View** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Offline Mode** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (via IndexedDB) |
| **Export** | CSV/PDF/Excel | CSV/PDF | CSV/XLS | CSV | ⏳ (need CSV/JSON) |

**Neuman Brain Advantages:**
- ✅ 100% free (no project limits)
- ✅ Privacy-first (local-first, no cloud required)
- ✅ Integrated with tasks/projects (no app switching)
- ✅ Open source

---

## 2. Data Architecture

### 2.1 Enhanced Data Model

Based on Toggl's proven structure with Neuman Brain enhancements:

```typescript
// Core Types (extend existing if basic types exist)

interface TimeEntry {
  id: string;                    // UUID
  workspaceId: string;          // Always use default workspace for now
  projectId?: string;           // Optional: Link to project
  taskId?: string;              // Optional: Link to Kanban task
  description: string;          // Title/what you worked on (required)
  notes?: string;               // Additional details

  tags: string[];               // Categorization (e.g., "meeting", "coding", "research")

  // Time tracking
  startTime: string;            // ISO 8601 timestamp
  endTime?: string;             // ISO 8601 timestamp (null = running timer)
  duration: number;             // Seconds (calculated or manual)

# Calendar Duplication Analysis: Time Tracking vs Planning

## Executive Summary

Two distinct calendar implementations exist in the codebase:
1. **TimeEntryCalendar** - Time Tracking feature (Phase 3.4)
2. **Calendar Widget** - Planning page

These calendars serve different primary purposes but share significant overlap in functionality. This analysis identifies opportunities for consolidation while preserving unique features.

---

## 1. Current State Analysis

### 1.1 TimeEntryCalendar Component
**Location:** `D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\components\TimeEntryCalendar.tsx`

**Primary Purpose:** Visualize time tracking entries in a monthly calendar format

**Data Sources:**
- `useTimeTrackingStore` - Zustand store for time entries and projects
- IndexedDB via `timeTrackingDb` for persistent storage
- Filters: date range, project IDs, tags, search query

**Rendering Logic:**
- Monthly grid view only (no week/day/agenda views)
- Displays time entries as colored blocks (project color-coded)
- Shows up to 4 entries per day, with "+N more" overflow indicator
- Day-level duration totals displayed
- Project legend showing all active projects with colors

**Unique Features:**
- **Time entry blocks** with project colors, descriptions, start times, and durations
- **Project-based color coding** (entries inherit project colors)
- **Duration summaries** (per-day totals in HH:MM format)
- **Tooltip details** showing full entry info (description, project, time range, duration)
- **Click-to-edit** functionality (opens EditTimeEntryModal)
- **Project legend** at bottom

**Interactions:**
- Click entry → `onEditEntry(entry)` callback
- Month navigation: Previous/Next/Today buttons
- Loads data for entire month on mount/navigation

**Technical Details:**
- Uses `formatDuration()` and `formatTime()` from `utils/timeFormatters.ts`
- Date grouping: Groups entries by `YYYY-MM-DD` date string
- 7-column grid (Sun-Sat), 4-6 rows depending on month
- Highlights current day with accent color border

---

### 1.2 Calendar Widget
**Location:** `D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\src\widgets\Calendar.tsx`

**Primary Purpose:** Event scheduling and task timeline visualization

**Data Sources:**
- `useCalendarStore` - Zustand store for calendar events
- `useKanbanStore` - Integration with Kanban tasks for date range bars
- Synced storage (localStorage with custom sync layer)

**Rendering Logic:**
- **Four view modes:**
  - Monthly: Grid with events and task bars
  - Weekly: Time-slot based (24-hour grid)
  - Daily: Single day with 24-hour timeline
  - Agenda: List view of upcoming events

**Unique Features:**
- **Calendar events** (title, description, time, location, reminders)
- **Recurring events** (daily/weekly/monthly with exceptions)
- **Multi-day events** (spanning multiple days with visual bars)
- **All-day events** vs timed events
- **Task integration** - Shows Kanban task date ranges as bars
- **Time slot interactions** - Click hour slot to create timed event
- **Reminders** - Configurable event reminders
- **Location field** - Geographic location for events
- **Print functionality** - Calendar print view

**Interactions:**
- Click day → Open event creation modal
- Click event → Edit/delete with recurring event handling
- View mode switching (Month/Week/Day/List)
- Task bars displayed alongside events

**Technical Details:**
- Uses `date-fns` for date manipulation
- Uses `getStandardDateKey()` from `utils/dateUtils.ts`
- Expands recurring events and multi-day events in memory
- Separate view components: `MonthView`, `WeekView`, `DayView`, `AgendaView`
- Month grid uses memoized day cells for performance

---

## 2. Overlap and Differences

### 2.1 Shared Requirements

| Feature | TimeEntryCalendar | Calendar Widget |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------|
| **Monthly grid view** | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Month navigation** | ✅ (Prev/Next/Today) | ✅ (Prev/Next) |
| **Day highlighting** | ✅ (Today) | ✅ (Today) |
| **Click interactions** | ✅ (Edit entry) | ✅ (Add/Edit events) |
| **Visual blocks on days** | ✅ (Time entries) | ✅ (Events) |
| **Color coding** | ✅ (By project) | ⚠️ (Single accent color) |
| **Overflow handling** | ✅ ("+N more") | ✅ ("+N more") |
| **Date utilities** | ✅ | ✅ |

### 2.2 Time Tracking-Specific Features

**Cannot be generalized:**
- Time entry data model (start/end times, duration in seconds)
- Project association and color inheritance
- Duration calculations and formatting
- Integration with `useTimeTrackingStore`
- IndexedDB persistence for time entries
- CSV export of time data
- Billable hours tracking

**Display requirements:**
- Show entry start time + duration on blocks
- Daily duration totals
- Project legend with color key
- Tooltip with full entry details
- Integration with EditTimeEntryModal

### 2.3 Planning-Specific Features

**Cannot be generalized:**
- Event data model (title, location, reminders, recurrence)
- Recurring event logic (daily/weekly/monthly patterns)
- Multi-day event expansion
- Task integration (Kanban date ranges)
- Week/Day/Agenda views
- Time slot-based interaction (hourly grid)
- Event reminders system

**Display requirements:**
- Timed vs all-day event distinction
- Multi-day event bars
- Task bars alongside events
- Current time indicator (in Day view)
- Multiple view modes

---

## 3. Data Architecture Comparison

### 3.1 Time Entry Model
```typescript
interface TimeEntry {
  id: string;
  workspaceId: string;
  projectId?: string;
  taskId?: string;
  description: string;
  notes?: string;
  tags: string[];
  
  // Time tracking specific
  startTime: string;      // ISO 8601
  endTime?: string;       // ISO 8601 (null = running)
  duration: number;       // Seconds
  
  billable: boolean;
  hourlyRate?: number;
  isPaused?: boolean;
  pausedAt?: string;
  
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### 3.2 Calendar Event Model
```typescript
interface CalendarEvent {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  
  // Event timing
  isAllDay?: boolean;
  startTime?: string;     // HH:MM format
  endTime?: string;       // HH:MM format
  endDate?: string;       // Multi-day end date
  
  // Recurrence
  recurrence?: RecurrencePattern;
  recurrenceId?: string;
  recurrenceException?: boolean;
  
  // Additional
  location?: string;
  reminders?: number[];   // Minutes before event
  
  // Multi-day rendering
  _isMultiDayPart?: boolean;
  _isMultiDayFirst?: boolean;
  _isMultiDayLast?: boolean;
}
```

### 3.3 Key Differences

| Aspect | TimeEntry | CalendarEvent |
|--------|-----------|---------------|
| **Time format** | ISO 8601 timestamps | HH:MM strings |
| **Duration** | Calculated in seconds | Inferred from start/end |
| **Recurrence** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| **Multi-day** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| **Project link** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No |
| **Location** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| **Reminders** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |

---

## 4. Shared Calendar Utilities

### 4.1 Date Utilities (Already Shared)
**Location:** `src/utils/dateUtils.ts`

Both calendars use:
- ✅ `getStandardDateKey()` / `getLegacyDateKey()`
- ✅ `isToday()`
- ✅ `getFirstDayOfMonth()`
- ✅ `getDaysInMonth()`
- ✅ `isDateBetween()` (for task ranges)

### 4.2 Time Formatters (Time Tracking Only)
**Location:** `src/utils/timeFormatters.ts`

Currently used only by TimeEntryCalendar:
- `formatDuration()` - HH:MM:SS formatting
- `formatTime()` - 12h/24h time formatting
- `formatTimeRange()` - Range display

**Opportunity:** Calendar Widget could use these for timed events

---

## 5. Consolidation Strategy

### 5.1 Recommended Approach: **Enhance Existing Calendar Widget**

**Rationale:**
- Calendar Widget already has multiple view modes (Month/Week/Day/Agenda)
- Has better architecture (separate view components, memoization)
- More extensible design (can show multiple data types)
- Planning page is the natural home for unified calendar

**Implementation:**
- Keep TimeEntryCalendar as-is (minimal refactoring risk)
- Add time entry visualization to Calendar Widget's MonthView
- Create unified data layer that merges events + time entries

### 5.2 Alternative Approach: **Create Base Calendar Component**

**Rationale:**
- Pure component with pluggable data adapters
- Could serve both use cases
- Better separation of concerns

**Drawbacks:**
- Higher initial implementation cost
- Risk of abstraction overhead
- May not justify complexity for 2 use cases

### 5.3 Hybrid Approach: **Unified Calendar Widget with Data Adapters** ⭐ RECOMMENDED

**Architecture:**

```
Calendar Widget (Planning Page)
├── MonthView
│   ├── Renders CalendarEvents (existing)
│   ├── Renders Task date ranges (existing)
│   └── Renders TimeEntries (NEW)
├── WeekView (existing)
├── DayView (existing)
└── AgendaView (existing)

TimeEntryCalendar (Time Tracking Page)
├── Simplified wrapper around Calendar Widget
├── Pre-configured to show only time entries
└── Time Tracking-specific controls
```

---

## 6. Proposed Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Data Layer Integration (Week 1)

**Goal:** Make Calendar Widget aware of time entries

**Tasks:**
1. Create `useCalendarData` composable hook
   ```typescript
   interface CalendarData {
     events: CalendarEvent[];
     timeEntries: TimeEntry[];
     tasks: Task[];
   }
   
   function useCalendarData(dateRange: DateRange): CalendarData {
     const { events } = useCalendarStore();
     const { entries } = useTimeTrackingStore();
     const { tasks } = useKanbanStore();
     
     return { events, timeEntries: entries, tasks };
   }
   ```

2. Add time entry filtering to Calendar Widget
   - Add `showTimeEntries` prop to MonthView
   - Add `showEvents` prop to MonthView
   - Allow selective display

3. Create time entry renderer component
   ```typescript
   function TimeEntryBlock({ entry, project }: Props) {
     // Renders time entry similar to current TimeEntryCalendar
     // Uses project color, shows duration, etc.
   }
   ```

### Phase 2: Visual Integration (Week 2)

**Goal:** Display time entries in Calendar Widget's MonthView

**Tasks:**
1. Modify `DayCell` component to accept both events and time entries
   ```typescript
   interface DayCellProps {
     // ... existing
     timeEntries?: TimeEntry[];
     projects?: TimeTrackingProject[];
   }
   ```

2. Add visual distinction
   - Events: Magenta/Blue colored bars (existing)
   - Time Entries: Project-colored blocks with duration badges
   - Tasks: Cyan/Yellow bars (existing)

3. Implement stacking logic
   - Max 2 events
   - Max 2 time entries
   - Max 2 tasks
   - "+N more" indicator

4. Add project legend (reuse from TimeEntryCalendar)

### Phase 3: Interaction Layer (Week 3)

**Goal:** Support editing both events and time entries

**Tasks:**
1. Add `onTimeEntryClick` callback prop
2. Create unified day modal
   ```typescript
   // When clicking a day:
   // - Show existing events
   // - Show existing time entries
   // - Allow adding events OR time entries
   ```

3. Add view mode toggle
   ```typescript
   enum CalendarDataType {
     All = 'all',
     EventsOnly = 'events',
     TimeEntriesOnly = 'time',
     TasksOnly = 'tasks'
   }
   ```

### Phase 4: Time Tracking Integration (Week 4)

**Goal:** Replace TimeEntryCalendar with Calendar Widget wrapper

**Tasks:**
1. Create `TimeTrackingCalendar` wrapper
   ```typescript
   function TimeTrackingCalendar() {
     return (
       <Calendar
         dataType="time-entries-only"
         showTimeSlots={false}
         onTimeEntryClick={handleEdit}
         showProjectLegend={true}
       />
     );
   }
   ```

2. Update TimeTracking page to use wrapper
3. Deprecate old TimeEntryCalendar component
4. Migration testing

### Phase 5: Advanced Features (Future)

**Goal:** Unified calendar experience across app

**Tasks:**
1. Add "Calendar" global view showing all data types
2. Add filter toggles (Show: Events | Time | Tasks)
3. Add density controls (Compact | Comfortable | Spacious)
4. Add drag-and-drop for time entry rescheduling
5. Add weekly/daily views for time entries

---

## 7. Technical Recommendations

### 7.1 Component Architecture

**Base Calendar Component:**
```typescript
interface CalendarProps<T extends CalendarItem> {
  items: T[];                          // Generic items (events, time entries, tasks)
  viewMode: CalendarViewMode;
  currentDate: Date;
  
  // Rendering
  renderItem: (item: T, date: Date) => ReactNode;
  getItemColor?: (item: T) => string;
  getItemTimeRange?: (item: T) => TimeRange;
  
  // Interactions
  onItemClick?: (item: T) => void;
  onDayClick?: (date: Date) => void;
  onTimeSlotClick?: (date: Date, hour: number) => void;
  
  // Config
  showLegend?: boolean;
  showTimeline?: boolean;
  density?: 'compact' | 'comfortable' | 'spacious';
}
```

### 7.2 Data Adapter Pattern

**Normalize different data types:**
```typescript
interface CalendarItem {
  id: string;
  date: Date;
  title: string;
  color?: string;
  timeRange?: { start: string; end: string };
  type: 'event' | 'time-entry' | 'task';
  metadata: any; // Original item
}

function adaptTimeEntry(entry: TimeEntry, project?: Project): CalendarItem {
  return {
    id: entry.id,
    date: new Date(entry.startTime),
    title: entry.description,
    color: project?.color,
    timeRange: {
      start: formatTime(new Date(entry.startTime)),
      end: entry.endTime ? formatTime(new Date(entry.endTime)) : undefined
    },
    type: 'time-entry',
    metadata: entry
  };
}
```

### 7.3 Performance Considerations

**Current Performance:**
- TimeEntryCalendar: Loads 1 month of entries (~30-200 items)
- Calendar Widget: Expands recurring events (potentially 100s)

**Optimizations:**
1. **Virtual scrolling** for long months (>100 items/day)
2. **Lazy loading** - Only load visible month
3. **Memoization** - Expensive calculations (recurrence expansion)
4. **Debouncing** - Month navigation
5. **Pagination** - Agenda view

---

## 8. Migration Path

### 8.1 Zero-Downtime Migration

**Strategy:** Gradual enhancement, not replacement

**Steps:**
1. ✅ **Week 1-2:** Add time entry support to Calendar Widget (Planning page only)
2. ✅ **Week 3:** Test with users, gather feedback
3. ✅ **Week 4:** Add toggle in Settings: "Use unified calendar in Time Tracking"
4. ✅ **Week 5-6:** Beta period, monitor for issues
5. ✅ **Week 7:** Default to unified calendar, keep old as fallback
6. ✅ **Week 8+:** Remove old TimeEntryCalendar after 2 weeks of stability

### 8.2 Feature Parity Checklist

Before deprecating TimeEntryCalendar, ensure Calendar Widget has:
- ✅ Time entry visualization (project colors, durations)
- ✅ Project legend
- ✅ Click-to-edit time entries
- ✅ Daily duration totals
- ✅ Month navigation
- ✅ Today button
- ✅ Tooltip with entry details
- ✅ Overflow handling ("+N more")

---

## 9. Benefits of Consolidation

### 9.1 User Benefits
- **Unified experience** - Same calendar UI across features
- **Cross-feature visibility** - See events AND time entries together
- **Better context** - "I have a meeting (event) from 2-3pm, then logged 3h of coding (time entry)"
- **Reduced cognitive load** - One mental model for calendar

### 9.2 Developer Benefits
- **Single source of truth** for calendar rendering
- **Easier maintenance** - Fix bugs once, applies everywhere
- **Reusable components** - DayCell, WeekView, etc.
- **Consistent behavior** - Date handling, navigation, etc.
- **Easier testing** - Shared test suites

### 9.3 Product Benefits
- **Feature velocity** - New calendar features benefit all views
- **Professional polish** - Consistent UX reduces "Frankenstein app" feel
- **Competitive advantage** - Unified time/event tracking is rare
- **Monetization** - "Advanced calendar" as premium feature

---

## 10. Risks and Mitigation

### 10.1 Technical Risks

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **Performance degradation** (100+ items/day) | High | Medium | Virtual scrolling, lazy loading |
| **Data corruption** during migration | Critical | Low | Backup stores, gradual rollout |
| **Feature regression** (losing TimeEntry UX) | High | Medium | Feature parity checklist, A/B testing |
| **Store conflicts** (Calendar vs TimeTracking) | Medium | Low | Clear data ownership, adapters |

### 10.2 UX Risks

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **User confusion** (events vs time entries) | Medium | High | Clear visual distinction, color coding |
| **Information overload** (too much on one view) | Medium | Medium | Filter toggles, density controls |
| **Lost workflows** (current TimeEntry users) | High | Low | Feature parity, toggle to old view |

---

## 11. Conclusion

### 11.1 Recommendation: **Unified Calendar Widget with Adapters**

**Verdict:** Extend the existing Calendar Widget to support time entries rather than creating a base abstraction.

**Key Decisions:**
1. ✅ **Keep Planning Calendar as foundation** - It's more feature-rich
2. ✅ **Add time entry rendering to MonthView** - Via adapter pattern
3. ✅ **Gradual migration** - Feature flag, beta testing, fallback
4. ✅ **Preserve TimeEntryCalendar short-term** - Low-risk safety net

### 11.2 Success Metrics

**Phase 1 (Month 1):**
- [ ] Calendar Widget can display time entries
- [ ] Visual distinction clear (events vs time vs tasks)
- [ ] Performance: <100ms render for 30-day month

**Phase 2 (Month 2):**
- [ ] Time Tracking page uses unified calendar
- [ ] 90% feature parity with old TimeEntryCalendar
- [ ] User feedback: 4+ star rating

**Phase 3 (Month 3+):**
- [ ] Old TimeEntryCalendar deprecated
- [ ] Bundle size reduced by ~15KB (removed duplicate logic)
- [ ] 0 regressions in time tracking workflows

### 11.3 Next Steps

1. **Review with team** - Validate architecture decisions
2. **Create implementation task** in `tasks/consolidate-calendar-views.md`
3. **Design mockups** - Unified calendar with all 3 data types
4. **Spike: Data adapter** - Prove concept with 1-day implementation
5. **Schedule work** - 4-week sprint with clear milestones

---

## Appendix A: File Paths Reference

**Time Tracking:**
- Component: `src/components/TimeEntryCalendar.tsx`
- Store: `src/stores/useTimeTrackingStore.ts`
- Page: `src/pages/TimeTracking.tsx`
- Database: `src/db/timeTrackingDb.ts`

**Planning:**
- Widget: `src/widgets/Calendar.tsx`
- Views: `src/components/{WeekView,DayView,AgendaView}.tsx`
- Store: `src/stores/useCalendarStore.ts`
- Page: `src/pages/Planning.tsx`

## Appendix B: Code Comparison

### Month Grid Rendering

**TimeEntryCalendar (lines 95-101):**
```typescript
// Create calendar grid
const calendarDays: (number | null)[] = [];
for (let i = 0; i < firstDayOfWeek; i++) {
  calendarDays.push(null); // Empty cells before month starts
}
for (let day = 1; day <= daysInMonth; day++) {
  calendarDays.push(day);
}
```

**Calendar Widget MonthView (lines 875-919):**
```typescript
// Generate day cells
const dayCells = useMemo(() => {
  const cells = [];
  
  // Previous month days
  for (let i = firstDay - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    const dayNum = daysInPrevMonth - i;
    const date = new Date(year, month - 1, dayNum);
    cells.push({ date, dayNum, isOtherMonth: true, ... });
  }
  
  // Current month days
  for (let i = 1; i <= daysInMonth; i++) {
    const date = new Date(year, month, i);
    cells.push({ date, dayNum: i, isOtherMonth: false, ... });
  }
  
  // Next month days (to fill grid)
  // ...
  
  return cells;
}, [year, month, ...]);
```

**Analysis:** Calendar Widget has better architecture (memoization, Date objects), but same core logic.
